- Screen: Lower brightness, enable auto-brightness, shorten auto‑lock, use dark mode (helps most OLED phones).
- Connectivity: Prefer Wi‑Fi over cellular; disable 5G when coverage is weak; turn off Bluetooth/GPS when not needed; set Location to “While Using” for most apps.
- Streaming/live: Reduce stream quality/bitrate, close other apps before going live, and plug in during streams when possible.
- Commute/offline: Download podcasts/music/maps over Wi‑Fi to use offline and save battery/data.
- Power modes: Use Low Power/Battery Saver when under ~30% or during long days.
- App tweaks: Disable auto‑play videos and limit widgets/live wallpapers.
- Charging/heat: Avoid high temps; don’t game/stream while charging; partial charges (20–80%) are gentler than deep cycles.
- Signal dead zones: Use Airplane Mode in no‑signal areas to stop constant searching.
- Battery health: If your battery health/capacity is significantly degraded, consider a replacement.
- On the go: Since you have a power bank/tech pouch, keep it topped up and in your kit for long shoots or travel days.

- Neck feel: Compare 50s vs 60s necks, fret size, and radius. Pick what feels best for fingerstyle comfort.
- Scale/tension: 24.75" (LP) feels slinkier than 25.5" (Strat). Down-tuning benefits from heavier strings—ask to try or set up for 12s if possible.
- Weight/balance: Les Pauls can be heavy—play standing with a strap for 10 minutes to check shoulder comfort and balance.
- Setup readiness: Check action, intonation, and nut slots. Ask if they’ll include a setup for open D and heavier gauges.
- Hardware: Test tuning stability (bend, retune), feel the bridge, check fret ends and general build quality.
- Amp parity: Demo through an amp similar to yours and use your typical gain/EQ so the test reflects your real sound.
- Practicalities: If considering used, inspect neck straightness and fret wear. Confirm return window and included post-purchase setup.
